name: search_tweets
description: Search for tweets from the last 7 days matching a query. The query supports Twitter search operators such as 'from:username', 'has:media', 'is:retweet', '-is:reply', 'lang:en', and boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT). Returns up to 100 tweets per request with pagination support.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string. Supports Twitter operators like 'from:', 'has:', 'is:', boolean logic, and quoted exact phrases. Maximum 512 characters.

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per request. Value between 10 and 100. Default is 10.

parameters.name: tweet.fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of tweet fields to return. Options include: created_at, author_id, public_metrics, source, lang, entities.

parameters.name: next_token
parameters.description: Pagination token from a previous response to retrieve the next page of results.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. Only return tweets created after this time. Must be within the last 7 days.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. Only return tweets created before this time.

data.text: Just tried the new OpenAI model and it's incredible for code generation!
OpenAI released a new API endpoint for embeddings. Here's my quick benchmark...
